title: Browser Displays Page with HTTP 500 - Internal server error
breadcrumbs: Browser Displays Page with HTTP 500 - Internal server error
text: Browser Displays Page with HTTP 500 - Internal server error If the browser displays a red error page, see Browser Displays a Red Error Page for more information. In any other case, depending on the specific error, here are some possible causes and resolutions. Possible cause Resolution Wrong jar file is used. Report in error.log file for example, "No middleware module found". Use the latest wrd*.jar file (for example, for V8.2.02, use wrd2014.jar ). In the web server, specify in JavaClassPath , with correct location. Stop and restart the web server. Try Server Page again. If error page is still displayed, try another Server Page or enter a few spaces after the Server Page name and press Enter. If this succeeds, try the previous Server Page. wrd20xx.jar is not in the correct location, specified in the web server. The web server .log file has not reported this error. Put the .jar file in correct location, or specify correct location in the web server's JavaClassPath . Stop and restart the web server. Try Server Page again. If error page is still displayed, try another Server Page or enter a few spaces after the Server Page name and then press Enter. If this succeeds, try the previous Server Page. The string UV7 in the MIDDLEWARE setting or the URD Servlet Alias is incorrect. error.log file reports, for example, "Couldn’t connect to application server: The Uniface Application Server cannot be started due to wrong logon information specified in the MIDDLEWARE setting. Specify correct UV8 string. Stop and restart the web server. Try Server Page again. No module in MIDDLEWARE string The error.log file reports (getServlet "wrong") jakarta.servlet. ServletException: No middleware module found. Specify MIDDLEWARE=UV8:Host| ... Stop and restart the web server. Try Server Page again. The string localhost in the MIDDLEWARE setting or the WRD Servlet is incorrect. The error.log file reports, for example, "Couldn’t connect to application server: Invalid host specified in the MIDDLEWARE setting. The system returned: "invalid host name"". Specify correct Host string. Stop and restart the web server. Try Server Page again. The username in the MIDDLEWARE setting of the WRD Servlet is incorrect. The error.log file reports, for example, "Couldn’t connect to application server: Cannot connect to the Uniface process on the remote address/port. The system returned: "Bad connection parameters"". Specify correct username and password. Stop and restart the web server. Try Server Page again. The RESOURCEDIR setting of the WRD Servlet is not set. The error.log file reports, for example, No resource directory set. Specify correct RESOURCEDIR for servlet alias. Stop and restart the web server. Try Server Page again. The ERRORDIR setting of the WRD Servlet is not set. The error.log file reports, for example, No error directory set. Specify correct ERRORDIR for servlet alias. Stop and restart the web server. Try Server Page again. The WEBAPPURL setting of the WRD Servlet is not set. The error.log file reports, for example, No web application URL set. Specify correct WEBAPPURL for servlet alias. Stop and restart the web server. Try Server Page again. The classname com.rocketsoftware.uniface.urd.WRDServlet has not been correctly defined. Specify correct definition in the Servlet Class setting. Stop and restart the web server. Try Server Page again. The WRD servlet is not defined as an Alias. Specify WRD servlet as a Servlet Alias. Stop and restart the web server. Try Server Page again. The username in the MIDDLEWARE setting of the WRD Servlet Alias is not present and one symbol ‘|’. The error.log file reports, for example, “Couldn’t connect to application server: Invalid format of the MIDDLEWARE setting". Specify correct username and password in Middleware string in the format UV8: Hostname + Portnumber | Username | Password | UST . Stop and restart the web server. Try Server Page again. RESOURCETIMEOUT value is 0 or negative. The error.log file reports, for example, (getServlet "wrong") jakarta.servlet.ServletException: The value of setting RESOURCETIMEOUT is invalid. Specify a valid RESOURCETIMEOUT value > 0 or remove setting and use default value for servlet. Stop and restart the web server. Try Server Page again. The RESOURCEDIR setting of the WRD Servlet Alias is set to a non-existing directory. The error.log file reports, for example, (getServlet "wrong") jakarta.servlet.ServletException: Given resource directory does not exist. Specify correct RESOURCEDIR pointing to existing correct location for servlet alias. Stop and restart the web server. Try Server Page again. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
